title: Mesembryanthemum elineatum
taxonomy: Mesembryanthemum elineatum is classified within the family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the ice plant or succulent family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. The Aizoaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Ruschioideae, which Mesembryanthemum elineatum is a part of. The Ruschioideae subfamily is known for its succulent plants, many of which are adapted to survive in dry, arid environments. Mesembryanthemum elineatum's classification is an example of the ongoing process of botanical discovery and classification.

intro: Mesembryanthemum elineatum, also known as the ice plant, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its unique, fleshy leaves and vibrant flowers. The health benefits of Mesembryanthemum elineatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include mesembrenone, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mesembryanthemum elineatum include topical creams and ointments for wound care, as well as dietary supplements in capsule or powder form for internal use.
